pages.raw_text:
endorsements were not completed
but were referred to Joint COM
mittee of farmers and organized
labor. which will meet Wednesday
night Of next week in the Hutton
building to complete the selections.


Filings by democrats to be made
this afternoon will be considered at
that time and any material worth
looking into that has been turned in
since noon today will be taken care
of.


X. b. Martin, president of the
club, presided over the meeting and
Frank Gould of the cooks and wait
ers' was secretary. Inspiring talks
in favor of good government were
made by a number of farmers. who
told the committee from the Cen
trail Labor council that the farmers
arc for good government to man,
and will stand by the ticket named
today. whether they be democrats
or republicans. in the final race at
the polls. A. f. Veatch also deliv-
cred a short address.


1t was distinctly a progressive
insurgent gathering, called together
to protest against generation OF
ring rule in county affairs.


A resolution was adopted favorine
the adoption Of the amendment for
equal suffrage at the November
election


A campaign committee to work
for the election of the ticket named
today will be selected at meeting
of the joint committee next We'd
nesdav night.
